rulings.Case Title: IN RE: SUBARU OF AMERICA, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 22, 2018, Line 1, PETITIONER SUBARU OF AMERICA, INC.'S Notice Of Petition To Compel Arbitration. Subaru of America, Inc.'s (SOA's) petition to compel arbitration is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The Subaru Dealer Satellite Service Facility Agreement does not come within the exception to arbitration provided by the Motor Vehicle Franchise Contract Arbitration Fairness Act (MVFCAFA), because it is not a contract under which motor vehicles are both serviced and sold. Moreover, the service agreement is neither illegal nor unconscionable; both parties are sophisticates with bargaining power. On the other hand, the Putnam Subaru dealer agreement does fit the MVFCAFA exception, because it regards the sale and service of motor vehicles, and discontinuing the service agreement could conceivably be found to modify the dealer agreement. Thus, Putnam is compelled to arbitrate claims arising from the service agreement and SOA's petition is to that extent GRANTED. However, I DENY the part of SOA's prayer that would compel Putnam to dismiss its New Motor Vehicle Board protests, because discontinuing the service agreement might be found to modify the dealer agreement. I recognize that these rulings could lead to actions that are partially parallel, but they are what the law requires. The parties are encouraged to agree to stay either the arbitration or the Board proceedings. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for SOA is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS MARSONER ET AL VS. FCA US LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Deposition Attendance And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, JUNE 22, 2018, LINE 2 PLAINTIFFS THOMAS MARSONER and ANNABELLE DARAUYA'S Motion To Compel Deposition Attendance And Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge Dianne Peebles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Off-Calendar. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dpeebleslaw@comcast.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = DP

rulings.Case Title: QUIXEY, INC VS. RICHARD GREFORY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 22, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANT RICHARD GREFORY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Defendant Gregory's motion to set aside default and vacate judgment is GRANTED under CCP 473.5. The longstanding policy in California's courts is to decide controversies on their merits. (Berman v. Klassman (1971) 17 Cal.App.3d 900, 909.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MILLER MARITAL DEDUCTION TRUST VS. ZURICH AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 22, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANT ZURICH AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The insurers' anti-SLAPP motion to strike is DENIED. The insurers meet prong 1 of the anti-SLAPP statute. Plaintiffs' complaint includes numerous references to the insurers' communications with counsel during the federal action and asserts them as a basis for liability. (Complaint, pars. 9, 103-112). Plaintiffs' complaint does more than make collateral or incidental allusions to protected activity and a bad faith action can be subject to the anti-SLAPP statute where the basis for liability is judicial communications. (See Seltzer v. Barnes (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 953, 968.) Plaintiff demonstrates a probability of prevailing on the merits and therefore, meets prong 2 of the anti-SLAPP statute. Paragraph 6 of the Stone declaration states that the insurers have not paid all required costs. In addition, that declaration and attachments show a conflict of interest that warrants the appointment of Cumis counsel. The insurers fail to show that the litigation privilege bars the entirety of plaintiffs' claims. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 22, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANT ALLIANZ INSURANCE COMPANY Joinder By Allianz Insurance Company In Defendant Zurich American Insurance Company S Special Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint Pursuant To Code Civ Proc 42516. The insurers' anti-SLAPP motion to strike is DENIED. The insurers meet prong 1 of the anti-SLAPP statute. Plaintiffs' complaint includes numerous references to the insurers' communications with counsel during the federal action and asserts them as a basis for liability. (Complaint, pars. 9, 103-112). Plaintiffs' complaint does more than make collateral or incidental allusions to protected activity and a bad faith action can be subject to the anti-SLAPP statute where the basis for liability is judicial communications. (See Seltzer v. Barnes (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 953, 968.) Plaintiff demonstrates a probability of prevailing on the merits and therefore, meets prong 2 of the anti-SLAPP statute. Paragraph 6 of the Stone declaration states that the insurers have not paid all required costs. In addition, that declaration and attachments show a conflict of interest that warrants the appointment of Cumis counsel. The insurers fail to show that the litigation privilege bars the entirety of plaintiffs' claims. Paragraphs 99-111 of the complaint allege sufficient facts to support the claim and Allianz raises a factual issue regarding whether it acted with malice, oppression and/or fraud within the meaning of CC 3294. rulings.Case Title: PETER ENGLANDER VS. WORLD AND MAIN (CRANBURY), LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Intervene
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 22, 2018, Line 9, APS&EE, LLC'S Motion For Leave To Intervene. A settlement of this Proposition 65 case is calendared for three court days from now, June 27. APS&EE, however, seeks to delay the proceedings with a motion to intervene. That motion is DENIED for two reasons, each independently sufficient. First, APS&EE's memorandum violates the 15-page limit. (CRC 3.1113(d)). Second, granting the motion would only increase costs for all parties and this Court. APS&EE concedes that (1) the relief sought by the proposed settlement is appropriate and (2) its only interest is in attorney fees and costs for a less-comprehensive Proposition 65 case it filed against the same defendant in Los Angeles. APS&EE cites no authority for applying CCP 387 to facts like these. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant World and Main is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)
